/mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90: 1121 - 1372
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


1121:       do  i2=2,n2-1


1122:          do  i1=2,n1-1


1123:             if( z(i1,i2,i3) .gt. ten( 1, 1 ) )then


1124:                ten(1,1) = z(i1,i2,i3)


[...]


1130:             if( z(i1,i2,i3) .lt. ten( 1, 0 ) )then


1131:                ten(1,0) = z(i1,i2,i3)


[...]


1137:          enddo


1138:       enddo


1139:    enddo


[...]


1331:          if( ten(i,ind) .gt. ten(i+1,ind) )then


1332: 


1333:             temp = ten( i+1, ind )


1334:             ten( i+1, ind ) = ten( i, ind )


1335:             ten( i, ind ) = temp


1336: 


1337:             j_temp           = j1( i+1, ind )


1338:             j1( i+1, ind ) = j1( i,   ind )


1339:             j1( i,   ind ) = j_temp


1340: 


1341:             j_temp           = j2( i+1, ind )


1342:             j2( i+1, ind ) = j2( i,   ind )


1343:             j2( i,   ind ) = j_temp


1344: 


1345:             j_temp           = j3( i+1, ind )


1346:             j3( i+1, ind ) = j3( i,   ind )


[...]


1357:          if( ten(i,ind) .lt. ten(i+1,ind) )then


1358: 


1359:             temp = ten( i+1, ind )


1360:             ten( i+1, ind ) = ten( i, ind )


1361:             ten( i, ind ) = temp


1362: 


1363:             j_temp           = j1( i+1, ind )


1364:             j1( i+1, ind ) = j1( i,   ind )


1365:             j1( i,   ind ) = j_temp


1366: 


1367:             j_temp           = j2( i+1, ind )


1368:             j2( i+1, ind ) = j2( i,   ind )


1369:             j2( i,   ind ) = j_temp


1370: 


1371:             j_temp           = j3( i+1, ind )


1372:             j3( i+1, ind ) = j3( i,   ind )

  • RecMII not computed since number of paths is unknown or > max_paths
  • Streams not analyzed since number of paths is unknown or > max_paths

Try to simplify control and/or increase the maximum number of paths per function/loop through the 'max-paths-nb' option.

This loop has 244 execution paths.

The presence of multiple execution paths is typically the main/first bottleneck.
Try to simplify control inside loop: ideally, try to remove all conditional expressions, for example by (if applicable):
  • hoisting them (moving them outside the loop)
  • turning them into conditional moves, MIN or MAX


Ex: if (x<0) x=0 => x = max(0,x) (Fortran instrinsic procedure)

      

  

    
    
      
  • gain
    • 
      
  • potential
    • 
      
  • hint
    • 
      
  • expert
    • 
    

  

  

  


Code clean check


Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation).
By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 123.33 to 8.00 cycles (15.42x speedup).

Workaround


  • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
  • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)
  • To reference allocatable arrays, use "allocatable" instead of "pointer" pointers or qualify them with the "contiguous" attribute (Fortran 2008)
  • For structures, limit to one indirection. For example, use a_b%c instead of a%b%c with a_b set to a%b before this loop


Vectorization


Your loop is not vectorized.
Only 7% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions).
By vectorizing your loop, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 123.33 to 13.54 cycles (9.11x speedup).

Details


All SSE/AVX instructions are used in scalar version (process only one data element in vector registers).
Since your execution units are vector units, only a vectorized loop can use their full power.


Workaround


  • Try another compiler or update/tune your current one
  • Remove inter-iterations dependences from your loop and make it unit-stride:

    • If your arrays have 2 or more dimensions, check whether elements are accessed contiguously and, otherwise, try to permute loops accordingly:
Fortran storage order is column-major: do i do j a(i,j) = b(i,j) (slow, non stride 1) => do i do j a(j,i) = b(i,j) (fast, stride 1)
      

    • If your loop streams arrays of structures (AoS), try to use structures of arrays instead (SoA):
do i a(i)%x = b(i)%x (slow, non stride 1) => do i a%x(i) = b%x(i) (fast, stride 1)


Execution units bottlenecks


Performance is limited by:

  • reading data from caches/RAM (load units are a bottleneck)
  • writing data to caches/RAM (the store unit is a bottleneck)

By removing all these bottlenecks, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 123.33 to 67.67 cycles (1.82x speedup).


Workaround


  • Read less array elements
  • Write less array elements
  • Provide more information to your compiler:

    • hardcode the bounds of the corresponding 'for' loop



  

No data for this section

  


Complex instructions


Detected COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS.


Details


These instructions generate more than one micro-operation and only one of them can be decoded during a cycle and the extra micro-operations increase pressure on execution units.

  • IDIV: 2 occurrences►

    • /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121
    • /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121


Conversion instructions


Detected expensive conversion instructions.

Details


  • CQTO: 2 occurrences►

    • /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121
    • /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121


Workaround


Avoid mixing data with different types. In particular, check if the type of constants is the same as array elements.

Type of elements and instruction set


No instructions are processing arithmetic or math operations on FP elements. This loop is probably writing/copying data or processing integer elements.

Matching between your loop (in the source code) and the binary loop


The binary loop does not contain any FP arithmetical operations.
The binary loop is loading 952 bytes.
The binary loop is storing 696 bytes.

Cycles and memory resources usage


Assuming all data fit into the L1 cache, each iteration of the binary loop takes 123.33 cycles. At this rate:

  • 8% of peak load performance is reached (7.72 out of 96.00 bytes loaded per cycle (GB/s @ 1GHz))
  • 8% of peak store performance is reached (5.64 out of 64.00 bytes stored per cycle (GB/s @ 1GHz))


Front-end bottlenecks


Found no such bottlenecks.

Function__nv_zran3__PARALLEL_F1L1106_9

          
Source file and linesmg.f90:1121-1372

        
Modulemg_omp_f90_aocc

        

The loop is defined in /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121-1124,1130-1131,1137-1139,1331-1346,1357-1372.

Analyzed code is defined in /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121-1123,1139,1338,1342,1346,1364,1368,1372.

Warnings:
  • Non-innermost loop: analyzing only self part (ignoring child loops).
  • Ignoring paths for analysis
  • Too many paths. If you really need to analyze all of the 244 paths individually, rerun with max-paths=244
  • RecMII not computed since number of paths is unknown or > max_paths
  • Streams not analyzed since number of paths is unknown or > max_paths

Try to simplify control and/or increase the maximum number of paths per function/loop through the 'max-paths-nb' option.

This loop has 244 execution paths.

The presence of multiple execution paths is typically the main/first bottleneck.
Try to simplify control inside loop: ideally, try to remove all conditional expressions, for example by (if applicable):
  • hoisting them (moving them outside the loop)
  • turning them into conditional moves, MIN or MAX


Ex: if (x<0) x=0 => x = max(0,x) (Fortran instrinsic procedure)

Code clean check


Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation).
By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 123.33 to 8.00 cycles (15.42x speedup).

Workaround


  • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
  • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)
  • To reference allocatable arrays, use "allocatable" instead of "pointer" pointers or qualify them with the "contiguous" attribute (Fortran 2008)
  • For structures, limit to one indirection. For example, use a_b%c instead of a%b%c with a_b set to a%b before this loop


Vectorization


Your loop is not vectorized.
Only 7% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions).
By vectorizing your loop, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 123.33 to 13.54 cycles (9.11x speedup).

Details


All SSE/AVX instructions are used in scalar version (process only one data element in vector registers).
Since your execution units are vector units, only a vectorized loop can use their full power.


Workaround


  • Try another compiler or update/tune your current one
  • Remove inter-iterations dependences from your loop and make it unit-stride:

    • If your arrays have 2 or more dimensions, check whether elements are accessed contiguously and, otherwise, try to permute loops accordingly:
Fortran storage order is column-major: do i do j a(i,j) = b(i,j) (slow, non stride 1) => do i do j a(j,i) = b(i,j) (fast, stride 1)
      

    • If your loop streams arrays of structures (AoS), try to use structures of arrays instead (SoA):
do i a(i)%x = b(i)%x (slow, non stride 1) => do i a%x(i) = b%x(i) (fast, stride 1)


Execution units bottlenecks


Performance is limited by:

  • reading data from caches/RAM (load units are a bottleneck)
  • writing data to caches/RAM (the store unit is a bottleneck)

By removing all these bottlenecks, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 123.33 to 67.67 cycles (1.82x speedup).


Workaround


  • Read less array elements
  • Write less array elements
  • Provide more information to your compiler:

    • hardcode the bounds of the corresponding 'for' loop



  

No data for this section

  


Complex instructions


Detected COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS.


Details


These instructions generate more than one micro-operation and only one of them can be decoded during a cycle and the extra micro-operations increase pressure on execution units.

  • IDIV: 2 occurrences►

    • /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121
    • /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121


Conversion instructions


Detected expensive conversion instructions.

Details


  • CQTO: 2 occurrences►

    • /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121
    • /mnt/lab/ihpc/aoc/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-OMP/MG/mg.f90:1121


Workaround


Avoid mixing data with different types. In particular, check if the type of constants is the same as array elements.

Type of elements and instruction set


No instructions are processing arithmetic or math operations on FP elements. This loop is probably writing/copying data or processing integer elements.

Matching between your loop (in the source code) and the binary loop


The binary loop does not contain any FP arithmetical operations.
The binary loop is loading 952 bytes.
The binary loop is storing 696 bytes.
